Home

Paper With Tables Formulas Figures And Refs

Alice — April 2026

Abstract

This fixture exercises figure/figcaption, simple tables, inline and block formulas (both with LaTeX annotations and MathML-only), and a references section. It is deliberately verbose to ensure the extracted text length clears the quality threshold.

1. Introduction

Inline math like the Pythagorean identity a2+b2=c2 a^2 + b^2 = c^2 appears in the middle of a paragraph. Block math is set off as:

E=mc2 E = m c^{2}

We also include a MathML-only formula without LaTeX annotation, which the system should preserve as MathML:

∫01x2dx

2. Architecture diagram

3. Experimental results

The benchmark numbers are summarized below.

Table 1: Accuracy comparison.
ModelTop-1Top-5
BaselineNet70.189.5
Ours82.495.3

4. Complex table

The following table uses rowspan and should fall back to HTML:

GroupMetric
F1AUC
A0.910.95
B0.880.92

5. Discussion

Section discussion. We analyze failure modes and scaling trends. Additional paragraphs of meaningful content would follow in a real paper, exploring implications across domains and laying out future directions.

References

  1. Alice et al., Paper One, NeurIPS 2024.
  2. Bob et al., Paper Two, ICML 2025.
  3. Carol et al., Paper Three, ACL 2025.
